audience: Self-employed professionals and SMEs considering a WordPress website or seeking to manage an existing site more effectively.
primaryDutchSearchIntent: KMO's helpen beoordelen wanneer WordPress geschikt is en hoe ze beheer, onderhoud, beveiliging en groei professioneel organiseren.

searchIntent.question: When is WordPress suitable for an SME, and how should hosting, ownership, updates, backups, security and maintenance be organised?
searchIntent.geographicContext: English-speaking SMEs, with VisualVibe services based in Limburg, Belgium.

directAnswer: WordPress suits SMEs that need to manage and regularly publish website content. It remains a strong choice only when the business clearly organises hosting, account ownership, controlled updates, recoverable backups, security, performance, plugin licences and ongoing maintenance.
title: WordPress for SMEs: complete guide to building and maintenance
metaDescription: Using WordPress for your SME? Learn when it fits and how to manage hosting, updates, backups, security, performance and ownership professionally.
factsToPreserve: WordPress is not suitable for every complex application or workflow.
Plugins add code, update obligations and possible conflicts.
The SME should control its domain, hosting and administrative accounts.
Updating everything without testing can cause problems, while never updating is riskier.
A backup is useful only when its location, retention and restore process are known and tested.
A security plugin does not replace sound account, access, update and hosting practices.
An SEO plugin does not compensate automatically for weak content or structure.
Total cost includes recovery, migrations, maintenance, licences and performance work, not hosting price alone.
